extension.description: Validates WCAG 2.2 (AAA) accessibility in HTML, JSX and TSX files using AccessMonitor rules. Shows diagnostics in the Problems panel and exposes tools for GitHub Copilot (MCP).
command.validateFile.title: A11y: Validate Current File
command.validateWorkspace.title: A11y: Validate Workspace
config.title: A11y MCP
config.wcagLevel.description: Minimum WCAG conformance level to enforce.
config.include.description: Glob patterns for files to validate.
config.exclude.description: Glob patterns for files to exclude from validation.
tool.validateFile.modelDescription: Validates accessibility issues in an HTML, JSX, or TSX file based on WCAG 2.2 (AAA) and AccessMonitor rules. Returns a list of violations with rule ID, WCAG criterion, severity (error/warning/notice), the offending element, line number, and a fix suggestion. Use this when you need to audit a specific file for accessibility problems.
tool.validateFile.input.filePath: Absolute or workspace-relative path to the HTML, JSX, or TSX file to validate.
tool.validateWorkspace.modelDescription: Scans all HTML, JSX, and TSX files in the workspace (or a specific sub-folder) for WCAG 2.2 accessibility violations. Returns a summary with total counts per severity and a per-file breakdown. Use this to get an overall picture of accessibility health across a project.
tool.validateWorkspace.input.folder: Optional workspace-relative folder to limit the scan scope. Defaults to the entire workspace root.
tool.validateWorkspace.input.maxFiles: Maximum number of files to scan. Defaults to 100.
tool.getRule.modelDescription: Returns the full definition of a specific WCAG or AccessMonitor accessibility rule: its description, affected WCAG success criterion, conformance level, default severity, fix guidance, and reference URL. Use this to explain a rule to the user or to get more context before fixing a violation.
tool.getRule.input.ruleId: The rule ID to look up, e.g. 'img_01b', 'title_02', 'aria_01', 'hx_03'.
tool.validateUrl.modelDescription: Fetches the HTML from a running local or remote server and validates it for WCAG 2.2 accessibility issues. Works best with server-side rendered pages (Next.js, Remix, Nuxt). For client-side rendered SPAs (Vite, CRA) the server returns an empty shell, so results will be limited — a warning is shown in that case. Use this when the project is already running (e.g. after npm run dev) and you want to validate the rendered output.
tool.validateUrl.input.url: Full URL to fetch and validate, e.g. http://localhost:3000 or http://localhost:5173/about.
